bioHotLightweight Curation — AI selection & significance rating
← bioHot

Metagenomics & Microbiome Informatics

Computational methods for metagenomic assembly, binning, taxonomic profiling, and functional annotation of microbial communities.

57 papers curated · High: 1 · Medium: 50 · Low: 6 · Updated: 2026-07-10

mSystems 2026-Jun-30 Medium

Signatures in the gut microbiome of German elite athletes: insights from a matched-subgroup analysis.

This study compared the gut microbiomes of 148 German elite athletes with 108 healthy adults, finding taxonomic and functional features associated with elite athletic status. A matched-subgroup analysis controlling for age, sex, BMI, and diet revealed specific microbial signatures linked to intense physical training.

microbiomemetagenomicssports
Microbiome 2026-Jun-30 Medium

Uncovering transcriptional processes in microbial communities adapted to differing saline conditions in salt-weathered historic buildings.

This study investigates the transcriptional processes of microbial communities living on salt-weathered historic buildings, revealing how different salt conditions shape gene expression related to stress tolerance and pigment production. The findings provide molecular insights into how these halotolerant and halophilic communities survive and cause biodeterioration, which could inform conservation strategies for cultural heritage sites.

transcriptomicsmicrobiomemetagenomics
bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology 2026-Jun-26 Medium

Kente: A Graph-based Pangenomic Approach for Horizontal Gene Transfer Detection in Microbiomes.

Kente is a new graph-based pangenomic method that detects horizontal gene transfer in microbiomes by modeling structural mosaicism across genomes, overcoming limitations of fragmented assemblies and reference bias. It enables more accurate identification of HGT events in complex microbial communities.

metagenomicsmicrobiometool
bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology 2026-Jun-25 High

Quantitative detection of gut microbial eukaryotes with EukDetect2 reveals global distribution of commensal protists and association with distinct microbial community structure.

EukDetect2 is an updated computational tool that accurately detects and quantifies microbial eukaryotes (like protists) in metagenomic sequencing data, overcoming previous limitations of contamination and incomplete reference genomes. Using this tool, the authors discovered that commensal protists are globally distributed in the human gut and are associated with distinct bacterial community structures. This work provides a valuable new resource for studying the overlooked eukaryotic members of the gut microbiome.

metagenomicsmicrobiometool
mBio 2026-Jun-24 Medium

Detoxifying and depolymerizing microorganisms reveal intertwined guild collaborations in the gut microbiome of the generalist macro-algivorous fish Kyphosus cinerascens.

This study reveals that the gut microbiome of the herbivorous fish Kyphosus cinerascens relies on collaborative guilds of microorganisms to detoxify and depolymerize macroalgal biomass. The microbes work together to break down structurally complex polysaccharides and neutralize inhibitory polyphenols, highlighting a symbiotic network that enables efficient digestion of macroalgae.

microbiomemetagenomicsevolution
World journal of gastrointestinal pathophysiology 2026-Jun-22 Medium

Gut barrier dysfunction and multidrug-resistant bacterial translocation in adult critical illness: Mechanistic insights from a systematic review.

This systematic review examines how gut barrier dysfunction, microbiome imbalance, and immune dysregulation may allow multidrug-resistant bacteria to move from the gut into the bloodstream in critically ill adults. The authors find that while these factors are associated with bacterial translocation, current human evidence is insufficient to establish clear causal mechanisms. The review highlights the need for more rigorous studies to understand and prevent these dangerous infections.

microbiomeimmunologyclinical
World journal of experimental medicine 2026-Jun-20 Low

Letter to the Editor: Circadian and microbial misalignment in metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease - mechanistic insights and chronotherapeutic potential.

This letter discusses how social jetlag disrupts the timing of gut microbiota and host circadian rhythms, leading to 'temporal dysbiosis' that worsens metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease. The authors propose that chronotherapeutic approaches could restore alignment between circadian and microbial rhythms to improve liver health.

microbiomeclinical
bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology 2026-Jun-19 Medium

Soil Resistomes in a Tropical Watershed are Indirectly Structured by Bacterial Community Interactions with Soil Properties.

This study investigates how soil properties and bacterial community interactions shape antibiotic resistance genes (ARGs) in a tropical watershed, finding that soil resistomes are indirectly structured by bacterial community composition rather than directly by soil properties. Using metagenomics and 16S rRNA sequencing across diverse habitats from beach to mountaintop, the authors show that soil properties influence bacterial communities, which in turn determine ARG profiles. This work provides insights into the ecological drivers of antimicrobial resistance in tropical soils.

metagenomicsmicrobiomeevolution
Research square 2026-Jun-18 Medium

Eco-evolutionary dynamics of defense systems in mobile genetic elements: Cui bono?

This paper explores why mobile genetic elements (MGEs) like viruses and plasmids frequently carry defense systems, often at higher prevalence than host chromosomes. The authors propose eco-evolutionary dynamics where these systems benefit the MGEs themselves by protecting their own genomes or by manipulating host competition, challenging the view that MGEs are purely parasitic. The work synthesizes recent metagenomic findings to reframe the evolutionary roles of defense systems in microbial communities.

evolutionmicrobiologymetagenomics
bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology 2026-Jun-15 Medium

AI-Accelerated Structure Elucidation of Boavistamides A-C, Cyclic Depsipeptides from a Marine Filamentous Cyanobacterium Collected in Cabo Verde.

Researchers discovered three new cyclic depsipeptides (boavistamides A-C) from a marine cyanobacterium collected in Cabo Verde, using AI-assisted methods like GNPS molecular networking and database dereplication. The compounds, one containing a rare amino acid, were structurally characterized by mass spectrometry and NMR, and showed antiplasmodial activity. This demonstrates how AI can accelerate natural product discovery from marine microbes.

drug-discoverymethodologymicrobiology
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-08 Medium

CAMBRA caries risk stratification is associated with distinct salivary and supragingival plaque microbiomes in pre-orthodontic patients.

This study found that CAMBRA caries risk stratification in pre-orthodontic patients is associated with distinct microbial communities in saliva and supragingival plaque, with higher risk groups showing different bacterial compositions. The findings suggest that CAMBRA risk categories reflect underlying microbiome differences, which could inform personalized preventive strategies for orthodontic patients.

microbiomeclinicaldentistry
BMC oral health 2026-Jul-06 Medium

Association between salivary microbiota-related amino acid metabolic dysregulation and tacrolimus-induced gingival overgrowth following kidney transplantation.

This study investigates how changes in the oral microbiome, specifically disruptions in amino acid metabolism, may contribute to gum overgrowth caused by the immunosuppressant drug tacrolimus in kidney transplant patients. By analyzing salivary microbiota, the researchers identified metabolic dysregulation linked to this side effect, offering potential targets for prevention or treatment. The findings highlight a mechanistic connection between oral microbial imbalance and drug-induced gingival overgrowth.

microbiomemetabolomicsclinical
Clinical infectious diseases : an official publication of the Infectious Diseases Society of America 2026-Jul-06 Medium

Pre-treatment Gut Microbiome Diversity and Function Linked to Cytotoxic and Natural Killer Cell Immune Responses after N-803 Treatment in People with HIV.

This study found that the diversity and function of the gut microbiome before treatment with N-803 (an IL-15 superagonist) are linked to immune responses involving cytotoxic and natural killer cells in people with HIV. The findings suggest that the gut microbiome may influence the effectiveness of this latency-reversing treatment.

microbiomeimmunologyclinical
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-06 Medium

Gut microbiome dynamics and alcohol use outcomes during naltrexone treatment: a 12 week follow-up study.

This study tracked gut microbiome changes over 12 weeks in people with alcohol use disorder taking naltrexone, finding that shifts in bacterial composition and function correlated with drinking outcomes. The results suggest the gut-brain axis may influence treatment response, highlighting the microbiome as a potential target for improving AUD therapies.

microbiomeclinical
Journal of microbiology & biology education 2026-Jul-06 Low

Illuminating the "black boxes" of microbiome sequencing.

This paper describes a protocol for undergraduate lab courses that helps students understand the 'black boxes' of microbiome sequencing by evaluating communities of cultured bacteria. It addresses common learning gaps where students extract DNA but never see bacteria, and where sequence-to-taxonomy conversion remains opaque.

microbiomeeducationmethodology
Animal microbiome 2026-Jul-06 Medium

Using sequence-derived microbiome information from bulk tank milk samples to assess the influence of post-milking teat disinfection in New Zealand dairy herds.

This study used shotgun DNA sequencing of bulk tank milk to investigate how post-milking teat disinfection practices affect the bacterial composition of milk in New Zealand dairy herds. The findings provide insights into the relationship between farm management practices and milk microbiome, which could inform mastitis control strategies.

microbiomemetagenomicsmicrobiology
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-04 Medium

Grassland afforestation more than forestry intensification shapes soil multifunctionality via microbial compositional change under abiotic constraints.

This study shows that converting grasslands to eucalyptus plantations (afforestation) has a stronger negative impact on soil multifunctionality than simply intensifying existing forestry practices. The loss of soil function is driven by changes in microbial community composition, which are constrained by abiotic factors like soil conditions.

microbiomemicrobiologyecology
Communications biology 2026-Jul-04 Medium

Fermentative iron reduction by a psychrotolerant Clostridium-dominant consortium enriched from Antarctic penguin-impacted soils.

Researchers enriched a cold-tolerant microbial community from Antarctic penguin-impacted soils that reduces iron at 4°C through fermentation, dominated by Clostridium bacteria. The consortium completely converted iron within three months and produced the mineral magnetite, revealing a previously underappreciated microbial iron cycle in ice-free Antarctic soils.

microbiologymicrobiomeevolution
Behavioral and brain functions : BBF 2026-Jul-04 Low

The combined effects of probiotic and high-intensity interval training on memory function in high fat diet-fed rats.

This study found that combining the probiotic Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG with high-intensity interval training improved memory function in rats fed a high-fat diet, likely by reducing oxidative stress and improving metabolic parameters. The combination was more effective than either intervention alone.

microbiomeneuroscience
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-03 Medium

Bio-stimulants improve tomato growth by regulating the rhizosphere microbiome involved in phosphorus and nitrogen cycling.

This study shows that bio-stimulants rich in humic acid improve tomato growth by altering the rhizosphere microbiome, specifically enhancing microbial communities involved in phosphorus and nitrogen cycling. The findings provide mechanistic insight into how these environmentally friendly alternatives to chemical fertilizers promote plant growth.

microbiomeplantsmetagenomics
NPJ biofilms and microbiomes 2026-Jul-03 Medium

Temporal landscapes of the gut microbiota-host axis reveal mechanisms of age-related eggshell quality decline in laying hens.

This study combines 16S rRNA sequencing, metagenomics, metabolomics, and single-cell RNA sequencing to show that age-related shifts in the gut microbiota disrupt host-microbe crosstalk and eggshell mineralization in laying hens. The findings reveal that aging impairs rhythmic synchronization between gut microbes and host intestinal cells, leading to reduced eggshell quality.

microbiometranscriptomicsmetabolomics
Nature communications 2026-Jul-03 Medium

Viral modulation of sulfur-oxidizing bacteria drives organic carbon sink formation during primary succession in deglaciating ecosystems.

This study shows that as glaciers retreat, viruses infecting sulfur-oxidizing bacteria help drive the formation of organic carbon sinks in newly exposed soils. By analyzing microbial and viral communities across a 130-year chronosequence, the researchers found that viral lysis of these bacteria releases organic matter that accumulates over time. This work reveals a previously unrecognized role for viruses in shaping carbon storage during primary succession in deglaciating ecosystems.

microbiomemetagenomicsevolution
Medicine 2026-Jul-03 Medium

Causal relationships between gut microbiota, C-reactive protein levels and colorectal cancer: A Mendelian randomization study.

This study uses Mendelian randomization to investigate causal links between gut microbiota, C-reactive protein (CRP) levels, and colorectal cancer (CRC). The findings suggest that certain gut microbial taxa may influence CRC risk, partly through effects on CRP levels, providing insights into potential causal pathways.

microbiomecancermethodology
FEMS microbiology ecology 2026-Jul-03 Medium

Host-selective PMA-PCR enhances bacterial diversity recovery and lowers detection limits in plant microbiome profiling.

This paper presents a new method (host-selective PMA-PCR) that physically disrupts plant cells before adding a DNA-binding dye (PMA) to block host DNA amplification, thereby enriching bacterial sequences without needing prior sequence knowledge. The approach improves detection of low-abundance bacteria and recovers more diverse microbial communities from plant samples. It offers a simpler, less biased alternative to existing host DNA suppression techniques for plant microbiome studies.

microbiomemethodologyplants
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-02 Medium

The Kenyan Human Gut Virome Catalogue reveals extensive viral diversity and age-dependent community structure.

Researchers created the first comprehensive human gut virome catalogue for Kenya, addressing the underrepresentation of African populations in existing databases. The catalogue reveals extensive viral diversity and shows that the gut virome community structure changes with age.

microbiomedatabasegenomics
Mucosal immunology 2026-Jul-02 Medium

Bioinformatics roadmap for characterizing the gut microbiome to study its interactions and associations with the gut mucosal immune system.

This review provides a practical roadmap for using bioinformatics and machine learning to analyze gut microbiome sequencing data and study its interactions with the host mucosal immune system. It highlights how cost-effective sequencing and improved computational methods now make it easier to investigate microbiome-immune relationships in chronic diseases like cancer. The paper serves as a guide for researchers navigating the available tools and statistical approaches in this field.

microbiomeimmunologyreview
The Brazilian journal of infectious diseases : an official publication of the Brazilian Society of Infectious Diseases 2026-Jul-02 Medium

Microbiome analysis of Cystic Fibrosis sputum presents higher sensitivity than the conventional bacterial culture.

This study shows that analyzing the airway microbiome of cystic fibrosis patients using DNA sequencing detects more bacterial species than standard culture methods, and that microbiome data correlates with clinical factors like CFTR mutation and hospitalization status.

microbiomeclinicalmethodology
Bioinformatics (Oxford, England) 2026-Jul-01 Medium

VirBinn improves viral genome binning from metagenomic Hi-C through graph diffusion.

VirBinn is a new computational tool that uses graph diffusion to improve the recovery of viral genomes from metagenomic Hi-C data, which is challenging due to sparse contact signals. By enhancing within-genome connectivity, it reduces fragmentation of viral bins and improves binning accuracy.

metagenomicstoolmethodology
Ecology and evolution 2026-Jul-01 Medium

How Host Phylogeny, Diet, and Habitat Affect Gut Microbial Diversity in Wild Snakes.

This study analyzed fecal metagenomes from 73 wild snakes across 23 species in China to characterize gut microbial composition and assess the relative influence of host phylogeny, diet, and habitat. The dominant bacterial phyla were Proteobacteria, Bacteroidetes, Firmicutes, and Actinobacteria, and the findings highlight how ecological and evolutionary factors shape the snake gut microbiome.

microbiomemetagenomicsevolution
Respiratory research 2026-Jul-01 Medium

Alterations in the fecal virome and bacteriome-virome interplay in IPAH.

This study investigates changes in the gut virome (viral community) and its interactions with bacteria in patients with idiopathic pulmonary arterial hypertension (IPAH), a severe cardiovascular disease. The findings reveal distinct alterations in the fecal virome and disrupted bacteriome-virome interplay, suggesting that viral communities may contribute to the disease's pathophysiology.

microbiomemetagenomicsclinical
BMC microbiology 2026-Jul-01 Medium

Comparison of library preparation protocols and bioinformatic pipelines in high-throughput 16S rRNA gene sequencing.

This study systematically compared different library preparation protocols and bioinformatic pipelines for 16S rRNA gene sequencing, finding that both steps introduce significant biases that affect bacterial community profiling. The choice of protocol and pipeline can substantially alter inferred microbial composition and relative abundance estimates, highlighting the need for careful method selection and standardization in microbiome studies.

microbiomemethodologymetagenomics
Ticks and tick-borne diseases 2026-Jul-01 Medium

Qualitative profiling of the gut-specific chlamydial population in Ixodes ricinus ticks.

This study investigates the diversity of Chlamydiota bacteria in the guts of Ixodes ricinus ticks from Austria, finding that a significant proportion of ticks carry these bacteria, with some species potentially associated with Lyme disease spirochetes. The research provides new insights into the tick microbiome and its possible role in pathogen transmission.

microbiomemicrobiologyclinical
Microbial genomics 2026-Jul-01 Medium

Mapping potential pathogen profiling in cetacean blow: comparative insights from sequencing technologies.

This study used 16S rRNA gene sequencing on both short-read (Illumina) and long-read (PacBio) platforms to characterize bacterial communities in cetacean exhaled breath condensate, identifying potential pathogens via a specialized database. The findings reveal significant differences in microbial composition between sequencing technologies, highlighting the importance of platform choice for accurate pathogen profiling in marine mammal health monitoring.

microbiomemetagenomicstool
Bioinformatics (Oxford, England) 2026-Jul-01 Medium

A novel transformer model of protein domains for viral taxonomy classification.

This paper presents a novel transformer model that uses protein domain information to classify viruses into taxonomic ranks, even when they are novel at lower levels. The approach addresses the limitation of sequence-similarity methods, which struggle with highly divergent viruses, by leveraging domain architectures for broader taxonomic placement.

machine-learningdeep-learningmethodology
Statistics in medicine 2026-Jul-01 Medium

Novel Distance Regression for Repeated Outcomes With Missing Data: Applications to Longitudinal and Crossover Studies of Microbiome Beta-Diversity.

This paper introduces Edger, a new statistical method for analyzing microbiome beta-diversity in studies with repeated measures and missing data. Edger handles within-cluster correlations and missing values better than existing approaches, enabling more reliable analysis of how microbial communities change over time in response to treatments or host factors.

microbiomemethodologystatistics
Environmental microbiology 2026-Jul-01 Medium

Multi-Omics Profiling of the Scaphoideus titanus Yeast-Like Symbiont Guides the Bioinformatic Discovery of Related Fungal Symbioses in Insects.

This study profiles the yeast-like symbiont (YLS) of the leafhopper Scaphoideus titanus using multi-omics, revealing its metabolic capabilities and evolutionary role. The findings provide a bioinformatic framework to discover related fungal symbioses in other insects, highlighting the understudied importance of fungal endosymbionts in insect evolution and ecology.

genomicstranscriptomicsmicrobiome
The American journal of clinical nutrition 2026-Jul-01 Medium

High postprandial endotoxemia is associated with recurrence of cardiovascular events in patients with coronary heart disease: from the CORDIOPREV randomized clinical trial.

This study found that high levels of bacterial endotoxins in the blood after meals are linked to a higher risk of recurrent cardiovascular events in patients with coronary heart disease. The findings suggest that post-meal endotoxemia could be a target for dietary interventions to reduce cardiovascular risk.

microbiomeclinicalimmunology
Journal of medical microbiology 2026-Jul-01 Low

Suggestive associations between genetically predicted gut microbiota and endometriosis: a two-sample Mendelian randomization study.

This study used genetic data to investigate whether gut bacteria can causally influence endometriosis risk. The authors found suggestive associations between several gut microbial genera and endometriosis, but these did not survive strict multiple-testing correction, meaning the evidence for a causal link remains weak. The work highlights the need for larger studies to clarify whether the gut microbiome plays a role in endometriosis development.

microbiomegenomicsclinical
Current protocols 2026-Jul-01 Medium

High-throughput Profiling of Pseudouridines in Microbiome-derived Bacterial RNA.

This paper presents a new method for mapping pseudouridine modifications in bacterial RNA from complex microbiomes. The workflow combines bisulfite sequencing with a computational pipeline to achieve base-resolution, quantitative mapping from low-input samples.

transcriptomicsmicrobiomemethodology
Plant signaling & behavior 2026-Dec-31 Medium

Deciphering soybean-microbiome interactions: from rhizosphere dynamics to sustainable yield enhancement.

This review discusses how the soybean-associated microbiome in the rhizosphere regulates plant growth, nutrient uptake, and stress tolerance, with recent multi-omics approaches revealing that root exudates shape microbial communities. Understanding these interactions could lead to strategies for sustainable yield enhancement in soybean, an important crop for protein and oil production.

microbiomemetagenomicsplants
Gut microbes 2026-Dec-31 Medium

Genome-scale metabolic models predict diet- and lifestyle-driven shifts of ecological interactions in the gut microbiome.

This study introduces EcoGS, a metabolic modeling tool that predicts how diet and lifestyle affect ecological interactions (like competition and cooperation) between gut microbes. By applying it to human gut microbiome data, the tool reveals that shifts in nutrient availability can fundamentally alter microbial community dynamics.

microbiomemetabolomicstool
Data in brief 2026-Aug-01 Medium

Long-read whole-genome sequencing dataset of microbial communities from industrially and municipally impacted freshwater wetlands in South Africa.

This paper presents a long-read whole-genome sequencing dataset of microbial communities from South African freshwater wetlands impacted by industrial and municipal pollution. The dataset, generated using PacBio sequencing, provides a genomic resource for studying microbial responses to various anthropogenic contaminants.

metagenomicsmicrobiomegenomics
Data in brief 2026-Aug-01 Low

PacBio HiFi sequencing datasets of culture-enriched airborne microbial cave communities from dolomitic Sudwala Caves, South Africa.

This paper presents a new dataset combining air quality measurements with long-read PacBio HiFi metagenomic sequencing of airborne microbes from the Sudwala Caves in South Africa. The resource characterizes airborne microbial communities across different cave compartments and seasons, providing a baseline for understanding microbial life in subterranean karst ecosystems.

metagenomicsmicrobiomedatabase
Plant-environment interactions (Hoboken, N.J.) 2026-Aug-01 Medium

Rhizospheric Microbes and Nanoparticles Synergize to Enhance Plant Immune Responses.

This review proposes the concept of 'nanobiotic synergies,' where combining nanoparticles with beneficial soil microbes can boost plant immune responses and stress tolerance, offering a sustainable alternative to synthetic agrochemicals. It highlights how recent advances in nanotechnology and microbiome research can be integrated to strengthen plant defense systems.

microbiomeplantsreview
Data in brief 2026-Aug-01 Medium

16S rRNA gene sequencing dataset describing the diversity and structure of soil bacterial communities across four pesticide-free agroecological cropping systems of arable crops from the CA-SYS experiment between 2018 and 2021.

This dataset provides 16S rRNA gene sequencing data from soil bacterial communities in four pesticide-free agroecological cropping systems over four years. It includes 595 high-quality samples from 42 plots with varying tillage and nitrogen inputs, offering a resource for studying soil microbiome responses to sustainable farming practices.

microbiomemetagenomicsdatabase
Access microbiology 2026--01 Medium

Metagenome-assembled genomes from the temperate forest phyllosphere in Eastern Canada.

Researchers constructed 423 metagenome-assembled genomes (MAGs) from microbes living on tree leaves (phyllosphere) in Eastern Canadian temperate forests. The MAGs were mostly bacteria from three major phyla, with two archaeal genomes also recovered, providing a new genomic resource for understanding plant-microbe interactions in forest ecosystems.

metagenomicsmicrobiomeplants
Frontiers in cellular and infection microbiology 2026--01 Medium

Microbial dysbiosis and inferred functional profiling reveals the potential role of Methylobacterium in prostate cancer.

This study characterizes the prostate tissue microbiome in an Arab cohort with prostate cancer, finding that microbial dysbiosis and functional profiling point to a potential role for Methylobacterium in the disease. The research addresses a gap in understanding tumor-associated microbial signatures in understudied populations.

microbiomecancermetagenomics
Bioinformatics advances 2026--01 Medium

DeepTaxa: a hybrid CNN-BERT framework for 16S rRNA taxonomic classification.

DeepTaxa introduces a hybrid CNN-BERT model that improves species-level classification of 16S rRNA sequences by learning sequence features directly, overcoming limitations of alignment-based and k-mer methods. This deep learning approach fills a gap in 16S taxonomy tools, offering more accurate classification even with incomplete reference databases.

machine-learningdeep-learningmicrobiome
Frontiers in microbiology 2026--01 Medium

Exploring the Tenebrio molitor gut microbiota response to LDPE and PET: putative genetic indicators and methodological insights.

This study examines how the gut microbiome of mealworms (Tenebrio molitor) changes when fed low-density polyethylene (LDPE) and polyethylene terephthalate (PET) plastics. Using DNA sequencing, the researchers found that plastic diets shift the microbial community composition and enrich bacteria with genes potentially involved in breaking down these synthetic materials.

microbiomemetagenomicsmethodology
FEMS microbes 2026--01 Medium

Revealing the bacterial diversity and variation of white filamentous microbial mats in marine mangroves of Guadeloupe Island in relation to human activities.

This study uses 16S rRNA metabarcoding to characterize bacterial communities in white filamentous microbial mats from Guadeloupe mangroves, finding that overall diversity remains stable across sites with different human impact levels. The research provides a high-resolution analysis of these poorly understood communities, revealing how anthropogenic activities may influence specific bacterial groups.

microbiomemetagenomicsmicrobiology
Frontiers in immunology 2026--01 Medium

Metagenomic characterization of gut microbiota in rheumatoid arthritis-associated interstitial lung disease: taxonomic shifts and clinical correlations.

This study characterizes the gut microbiome in patients with rheumatoid arthritis-associated interstitial lung disease (RA-ILD), identifying specific taxonomic shifts compared to RA patients without lung disease. These microbial changes correlate with clinical parameters, suggesting that gut microbiota composition may serve as a diagnostic biomarker for RA-ILD.

microbiomemetagenomicsclinical
Frontiers in nutrition 2026--01 Medium

Integrated multi-omics analysis reveals distinct microbiota-metabolite signatures and a novel HCN2-2-hydroxybutyric acid interaction in inflammatory bowel disease.

This study profiles fecal short-chain fatty acids and gut microbiota in ulcerative colitis and Crohn's disease, identifying distinct metabolite-microbe signatures for each subtype. It further uncovers a novel interaction between the ion channel HCN2 and 2-hydroxybutyric acid, suggesting a potential molecular mechanism by which microbial metabolites influence host physiology in inflammatory bowel disease.

microbiomemetabolomicsimmunology
PloS one 2026--01 Medium

Key targets and mechanisms by which gut microbiota-derived metabolites regulate Alzheimer's disease through the immune - inflammatory pathway: Based on network pharmacology and molecular docking.

This study uses network pharmacology and molecular docking to identify gut microbiota-derived metabolites and their key targets (e.g., IL6, NFKB1, TLR4) that may regulate Alzheimer's disease through immune-inflammatory pathways. The findings suggest specific metabolites and pathways that could be targeted for therapeutic intervention.

network-analysisimmunologyneuroscience
Frontiers in cellular and infection microbiology 2026--01 Medium

Vaginal Acinetobacter infection as a cause of cervical collagenolysis.

This paper investigates how vaginal Acinetobacter infection may contribute to preterm birth by breaking down cervical collagen. The study suggests that specific microbial infections can directly weaken cervical tissue, offering a potential mechanism linking vaginal microbiota to preterm labor.

microbiomeclinicalimmunology
Computational and structural biotechnology journal 2026--01 Medium

Microbiome-Metabolome Crosstalk in HPV Pathogenesis: From Ecosystem Dynamics to Translational Biomarkers.

This review synthesizes metabolomic studies to show how high-risk HPV infection alters the cervicovaginal microenvironment through metabolic reprogramming, with distinct metabolic signatures (increased microbial amines and oxidative stress) associated with different stages of infection, persistence, and progression to cervical cancer.

metabolomicsmicrobiomecancer
PloS one 2026--01 Medium

A pilot randomized trial on the usability and acceptability of an app (MyIBDDiet) to improve the self-management of anti-inflammatory diet for individuals with inflammatory bowel disease: A protocol paper.

This protocol describes a pilot trial of a co-designed mobile app (MyIBDDiet) to help patients with inflammatory bowel disease self-manage an anti-inflammatory diet. The study will randomize 40 patients to test the app's usability and acceptability over 60 days, addressing a gap in formally evaluated digital tools for IBD dietary management.

toolclinicalimmunology
Genetics and molecular biology 2026--01 Low

Assessment of the bacterial community of the human upper respiratory tract in patients affected by Covid-19.

This study analyzed the bacterial community in the upper respiratory tract of COVID-19 patients and healthy controls, finding that SARS-CoV-2 infection is associated with changes in bacterial diversity. The results suggest that COVID-19 may alter the respiratory microbiota, which could influence disease progression and host-virus interactions.

microbiomeclinicalimmunology
